ddls:
CREATE TABLE "survey_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each survey response */,
  "heart_disease_or_attack" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has had a heart disease or attack */,
  "high_bp" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has high blood pressure */,
  "high_chol" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has high cholesterol */,
  "chol_check" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has had a cholesterol check */,
  "bmi" REAL /* Body Mass Index (BMI) of the respondent */,
  "smoker" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent is a smoker */,
  "stroke" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has had a stroke */,
  "diabetes" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has diabetes */,
  "phys_activity" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent engages in physical activity */,
  "fruits" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent consumes fruits */,
  "veggies" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent consumes vegetables */,
  "hvy_alcohol_consump" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent consumes heavy alcohol */,
  "any_healthcare" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has any healthcare */,
  "no_doc_bc_cost" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has no doctor due to cost */,
  "gen_hlth" INTEGER /* General health rating (1-5, with 5 being excellent) */,
  "ment_hlth" INTEGER /* Number of days of poor mental health in the past 30 days */,
  "phys_hlth" INTEGER /* Number of days of poor physical health in the past 30 days */,
  "diff_walk" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has difficulty walking */,
  "sex" INTEGER /* Gender of the respondent (0: Female, 1: Male) */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age group of the respondent (1-13, with 13 being 80+) */,
  "education" INTEGER /* Education level of the respondent (1-6, with 6 being Doctorate degree) */,
  "income" INTEGER /* Income level of the respondent (1-8, with 8 being $75,000+) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the survey response */,
  "survey_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey was completed */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the survey was conducted */,
  "survey_duration" TEXT /* Duration it took to complete the survey */,
  "survey_version" TEXT /* Version of the survey form used */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes from the respondent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_chol_check FOREIGN KEY ("chol_check") REFERENCES health_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_heart_disease_or_attack FOREIGN KEY ("heart_disease_or_attack") REFERENCES health_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_diabetes FOREIGN KEY ("diabetes") REFERENCES health_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_hvy_alcohol_consump FOREIGN KEY ("hvy_alcohol_consump") REFERENCES lifestyle_choices ("choice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_fruits FOREIGN KEY ("fruits") REFERENCES lifestyle_choices ("choice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_high_chol FOREIGN KEY ("high_chol") REFERENCES health_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_no_doc_bc_cost FOREIGN KEY ("no_doc_bc_cost") REFERENCES lifestyle_choices ("choice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_gen_hlth FOREIGN KEY ("gen_hlth") REFERENCES health_ratings ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_phys_activity FOREIGN KEY ("phys_activity") REFERENCES lifestyle_choices ("choice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_any_healthcare FOREIGN KEY ("any_healthcare") REFERENCES lifestyle_choices ("choice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_stroke FOREIGN KEY ("stroke") REFERENCES health_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_veggies FOREIGN KEY ("veggies") REFERENCES lifestyle_choices ("choice_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survey_responses_high_bp FOREIGN KEY ("high_bp") REFERENCES health_conditions ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the health condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the health condition */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the condition (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  "treatment" TEXT /* Information about the treatment or management of the condition */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lifestyle_choices" (
  "choice_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lifestyle choice */,
  "choice_name" TEXT /* Name of the lifestyle choice */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the lifestyle choice */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the lifestyle choice (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "intensity" TEXT /* Intensity level of the lifestyle choice (e.g., light, moderate, heavy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("choice_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "sex" INTEGER /* Gender of the respondent (0: Female, 1: Male) */,
  "age_group" INTEGER /* Age group of the respondent (1-13, with 13 being 80+) */,
  "education_level" INTEGER /* Education level of the respondent (1-6, with 6 being Doctorate degree) */,
  "income_level" INTEGER /* Income level of the respondent (1-8, with 8 being $75,000+) */,
  "race" TEXT /* Racial background of the respondent */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnic background of the respondent */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the respondent */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the respondent */,
  "number_of_children" INTEGER /* Number of children the respondent has */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health rating */,
  "rating_value" INTEGER /* Rating value (1-5, with 5 being excellent) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the health rating */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the health rating was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mental_health" (
  "mental_health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mental health record */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey response */,
  "poor_days_last_30_days" INTEGER /* Number of days of poor mental health in the past 30 days */,
  "depression" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has been diagnosed with depression */,
  "anxiety" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the respondent has been diagnosed with anxiety */,
  "stress_level" INTEGER /* Rating of the respondent's stress level (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mental_health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mental_health_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "physical_health" (
  "physical_health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each physical health record */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey response */,
  "poor_days_last_30_days" INTEGER /* Number of days of poor physical health in the past 30 days */,
  "chronic_conditions" TEXT /* List of chronic conditions the respondent has */,
  "allergies" TEXT /* List of allergies the respondent has */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("physical_health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_physical_health_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("response_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey response being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the survey response */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the survey response was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used to access the system (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_health_data" (
  "public_health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public health record */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey response */,
  "public_health_stat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the public health statistic */,
  "stat_value" REAL /* Value of the public health statistic */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the public health statistic is associated with */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the public health statistic was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("public_health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_health_data_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_public_health_data_public_health_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("public_health_stat_id") REFERENCES public_health_stats ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "public_health_stats" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each public health statistic */,
  "stat_name" TEXT /* Name of the public health statistic */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the public health statistic */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the public health statistic (e.g., percentage, count) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the public health statistic (e.g., CDC, WHO) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hospital_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hospital record */,
  "response_id" INTEGER /* ID of the survey response */,
  "hospital_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hospital */,
  "admission_date" TEXT /* Date of hospital admission */,
  "discharge_date" TEXT /* Date of hospital discharge */,
  "diagnosis" TEXT /* Diagnosis given during the hospital stay */,
  "emergency" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the hospital admission was an emergency */,
  "hospital_name" TEXT /* Name of the hospital */,
  "treatment_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the treatment during the hospital stay */,
  "discharge_status" TEXT /* Status of the respondent at discharge (e.g., recovered, transferred) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hospital_records_response_id FOREIGN KEY ("response_id") REFERENCES survey_responses ("response_id")
)
